title: quercus frainetto var. hungarica
intro: Quercus frainetto var. hungarica, also known as Hungarian pedunculate oak, is a subspecies of the pedunculate oak tree native to Eastern Europe. The leaves and bark of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus frainetto var. hungarica include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
